entries.definition:
كتاب الحاء

حشد - حشر

(حشر) الحاء والشين والراء قريبُ المعنى من الذي قبلِه، وفيه زيادةُ معنىً، وهو السّوق والبعثُ والانبعاث.

وأهل اللغة يقولون: الحشر الجمع مع سَوْقٍ، وكلُّ جمعٍ حَشْر. والعرب تقول: حَشرَتْ مالَ بني فلانٍ السنةُ كأنّها جمعته، ذهبت به وأتَتْ عليه. قال رؤبة:

وحْشٌ ولا طمشٌ من الطُّموشِ

وما نجا من حَشْرِها المحشوشِ

ويقال أُذُنٌ حَشْرَةٌ، إذا كانت مجتمِعة الخَلْق. قال:

كإعْلِيط مَرْخٍ إذا ما صَفِرْ

لها أذُنٌ حَشْرَةٌ مَشْرَةٌ

حَسَنَةٌ for those who do good there is a reward in this present world
II [quasi-act. part.] good (33:21) لَقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ you
have had in the Messenger of God a good model.

حُسْنَى ḥusnā I [elat. fem.; dual حُسْنَيَيْن husnayayn; masc. أَحْسَن
ʾaḥsan] better/best (7:180) وَلِلَّهِ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى فَادْعُوهُ بِهَا to God
belong the names most beautiful; call upon Him by them II [n.:
الْحُسْنَى al-ḥusnā] 1 the best [of rewards], interpreted as Paradise
(10:26) لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنَى وَزِيَادَةٌ for the good-doers is the reward
most fair and more besides; *(9:52) إِحْدَى الْحُسْنَيَيْنِ one of the two
best outcomes, (said to mean martyrdom and victory) 2 all that is
good, all that is being ordained by God, the true faith (92:6) وَصَدَّقَ
بِالْحُسْنَى and believes in the truth of what God has ordained.

حِسَان ḥisān [pl. of quasi-act. parts. fem. حَسَنَة hasanatun and
حَسْنَاء ḥasnãʾ] beautiful, fine (55:76) مُتَّكِئِينَ عَلَى رَفْرَفٍ خُضْرٍ وَعَبْقَرِيٍّ
حِسَانٍ they will be reclining on green cushions and fine carpets.

إِحْسَان ʾiḥsān [v. n./ n.] 1 good work, doing good deeds (55:60)
هَلْ جَزَاءُ الْإِحْسَانِ إِلَّا الْإِحْسَانُ shall the reward of goodness be
[anything] but goodness?! 2 uprightness, correctness, piety,
goodness, doing good (9:100) وَالسَّابِقُونَ الْأَوَّلُونَ مِنَ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنْصَارِ
وَالَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُمْ بِإِحْسَانٍ and the first vanguards of the emigrants (q.v.)
and the Helpers (q.v.) and those who followed them in
goodness/in doing good.

مُحْسِن muḥsin [act. part.; pl. مُحْسِنُون muḥsinūn; pl. fem.
مُحْسِنَات muḥsināt] one who does good, who is good, a charitable person, a
righteous person (33:29) فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِنَاتِ مِنْكُنَّ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا that God
has prepared for those of you who do good a great reward.
entries.definition_english:
حَسَنَةٌ for those who do good there is a reward in this present world
II [quasi-act. part.] good (33:21) لَقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ you

good, all that is being ordained by God, the true faith (92:6) وَصَدَّقَ
بِالْحُسْنَى and believes in the truth of what God has ordained.

حِسَان ḥisān [pl. of quasi-act. parts. fem. حَسَنَة hasanatun and
حَسْنَاء ḥasnãʾ] beautiful, fine (55:76) مُتَّكِئِينَ عَلَى رَفْرَفٍ خُضْرٍ وَعَبْقَرِيٍّ
حِسَانٍ they will be reclining on green cushions and fine carpets.

إِحْسَان ʾiḥsān [v. n./ n.] 1 good work, doing good deeds (55:60)
هَلْ جَزَاءُ الْإِحْسَانِ إِلَّا الْإِحْسَانُ shall the reward of goodness be
[anything] but goodness?! 2 uprightness, correctness, piety,
goodness, doing good (9:100) وَالسَّابِقُونَ الْأَوَّلُونَ مِنَ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنْصَارِ
وَالَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُمْ بِإِحْسَانٍ and the first vanguards of the emigrants (q.v.)
and the Helpers (q.v.) and those who followed them in
goodness/in doing good.

مُحْسِن muḥsin [act. part.; pl. مُحْسِنُون muḥsinūn; pl. fem.
مُحْسِنَات muḥsināt] one who does good, who is good, a charitable person, a
righteous person (33:29) فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِنَاتِ مِنْكُنَّ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا that God
has prepared for those of you who do good a great reward.

entries.definition:
h–sh–r insects; yield of the land; to gather together, to
assemble, to rally, to round up (of animals); to hone, to sharpen
up; to ruin. Of this root, five forms occur 43 times in the Qurʾan:
حَشَرَ ḥashara 19 times; حُشِرَ ḥushira 18 times; حَشْر ḥashr twice;
حَاشِرِين ḥāshirīn three times and مَحْشُورَة maḥshūratun once.

حَشَرَ ḥashara u [v. trans.; pass. حُشِرَ ḥushira] 1 to assemble, call
up for a gathering (79:23) فَحَشَرَ فَنَادَى فَقَالَ أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلَى so, he
gathered [his people], proclaiming, I am your supreme lord 2 to

212

حاش لله

array, to muster, to bring together (6:111) وَلَوْ أَنَّا نَزَّلْنَا إِلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةَ
وَكَلَّمَهُمُ الْمَوْتَى وَحَشَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ قُبُلًا مَا كَانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا إِلَّا أَنْ يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ
even if We sent the angels down to them, and the dead spoke to them,
and We gathered all things right before them, they still would not
believe, unless God so willed 3 to marshal, to bring for the final
Judgement (17:97) وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَى وُجُوهِهِمْ عُمْيًا وَبُكْمًا وَصُمًّا and
We marshal them, on the Day of Resurrection, lying on their
faces, blind, dumb and deaf.

حَشْر ḥashr [v. n./ n.] 1 assembly, gathering, raising from the
dead (50:44) يَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ الْأَرْضُ عَنْهُمْ سِرَاعًا ذَلِكَ حَشْرٌ عَلَيْنَا يَسِيرٌ
on the Day when the earth is split asunder from about them, [emerging] in
haste—that is a gathering easy for Us to muster; *الحشر name of
Sura 59, Medinan sura, so-named because of the reference in
verse 2 to the ‘Gathering of Forces’ 2 migration, evacuation,
exile (59:2) هُوَ الَّذِي أَخْرَجَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مِنْ دِيَارِهِمْ لِأَوَّلِ الْحَشْرِ it
was He who drove out those among the People of the Book who
disbelieved from their homes at the first exile [also interpreted as:
the first gathering of forces].

حَاشِرِين ḥāshirīn [pl. of act. part. حَاشِر ḥāshir] ones who call for
assembly, summoners (7:111) قَالُوا أَرْجِهْ وَأَخَاهُ وَأَرْسِلْ فِي الْمَدَائِنِ حَاشِرِينَ
they said, ‘Delay him and his brother for a while and send, into
the cities, summoners.’

مَحْشُورَة maḥshūratun [pass. part. fem.] gathered together,
assembled (38:19) وَالطَّيْرَ مَحْشُورَةً كُلٌّ لَهُ أَوَّابٌ and the birds, gathered
together; each to him reverting.

حَاشَ لِلَّهِ ḥāsha li ʾllāh (see ح/و/ش h–w–sh).
